concept:
Tested 3 ? Four causes of microcytic anemia:
TICS:
Thalassemia
Iron deficiency
Anemia of Chronic disease
Sideroblastic anemia
Elderly male with hypochromic, microcytic anemia is asymptomatic. Probable diagnosis? Diagnostic tests?
Probable diagnosis: Colorectal cancer
Diagnostic tests:
FOBT
Sigmoidoscopy
Precipitants of hemolytic crisis in patients with G6PD deficiency:
Sulfonamides
Antimalarial drugs
Fava beans
Most common inherited hemolytic anemia is: Hereditary spherocytosis
Diagnostic test for hereditary spherocytosis is: Osmotic fragility test
Pure RBC aplasia is from: Diamond-Blackfan anemia
Anemia associated with:
Absent radii and thumbs
Diffuse hyperpigmentation
Cafe-au-lait spots
Microcephaly
Pancytopenia
Faconi's anemia
Treatments and viruses that cause aplastic anemia:
TreatmentsViruses
Chloramphenicol
Sulfonamides
Radiation
Chemotherapeutic agents
HIV
Hepatitis
Parvovirus B19
EBV
How do you distinguish
primary polycythemia vera
secondary polycythemia vera
Both have increased HCT and RBC mass, but primary polycythemia vera should have normal oxygen saturation and low erythropoietin level.
Thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura (TTP) pentad is:
Anemia/Splenomegaly
Thrombocytopenia
Fever
Acute Renal Failure (ARF)
Neurologic changes (change in Mental Status)
Hemolytic-uremic syndrom (HUS) triad is:
Anemia
Thrombocytopenia
Acute Renal Failure (ARF)
Treatment for Thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura (TTP) is:
Emergent large-volume plasmapheresis
Corticosteroids
Antiplatelet drugs
Treatment for ITP in children:
Usually resolves spontaneously
May require IVIG and/or corticosteriods
Which of the following are elevated in DIC:
Fibrin split products
D-dimer
Fibrinogen
Platelets
HCT
Fibrin split products are elevated
D-dimer is elevated
Platelets are decreased
Fibrinogen is decreased
HCT is decreased
An 8 year-old boy presents with hemathrosis and increased PTT with normal PT and BT. What is the diagnosis and treatment?
Diagnosis: Hemophilia A or B
Treatment:
Consider desmopressin (for hemophilia A)
Factor VIII or IX supplements
A 14 year-old girl presents with prolonged bleeding after dental surgery and with menses, normal PT, normal and increased PTT, and increased BT. What is the diagnosis and treatment?
Diagnosis: von Willebrand's disease
Treatment:
Desmopressin
FFP
Cryoprecipitate
A 60 year-old African-American male presents with bone pain. Workup for multiple myeloma might reveal what?
Monoclonal gammopathy
Bence Jones proteinuria
"punched-out" lesions on X-ray of skull and long bones
Reed-Sternberg cells indicate: Hodgekin's lymphoma
With a patient who:
is 10 year-old
is a boy
has a fever
is experiencing weight loss
is experiencing night sweats
has an eam showing anterior mediastinal mass Suspected diagnosis is: non-Hodgekin's lymphoma
A patient with:
Microcytic anemia
Decreased serum iron
Decreased total iron-binding capacity (TIBC)
Normal or increased ferritin has: Anemia of chronic disease
A patient with:
Microcytic anemia
Decreased serum iron
Increased total iron-binding capacity (TIBC)
Decreased ferritin has: Iron-deficiency anemia
A patient:
is 80 year-old
is male
with fatique
with lymphadenopathy
with splenomegaly
isolated lymphocytosis Suspected diagnosis is: Chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL)
Late, life-threatening complication of chronic myelogenous leukemia (CML) is: A blast crisis consisting of:
Fever
Bone pain
Splenomegaly
Pancytopenia
Auer rods on a blood smear indicate: Acute myelogenous leukemia (AML)
Acute myelogenous leukemia (AML) subtype associated with DIC: M3
Electrolyte changes in tumor lysis syndrome are:
Decreased Ca2+
Increased K+
Increased phosphate
Increased uric acid
What is the treatment for Acute myelogenous leukemia (AML) M3? Retinoic acid
Patient is
50 year-old
male
with early satiety
with splenomegaly
with bleeding
with Cytogenetics showing t(9,22) Diagnosis? Chronic Myelogenous Leukemia (CML)
What are Heinz bodies? Intracellular inclusions seen in
Thalassemia
G6PD deficiency
postsplenectomy
Patient
with autosomal-recessive disorder
with a defect in the GPIIbIIIa platelet receptor
with decreased platelet aggregation has: Glanzmann's thrombasthenia
Virus associated with aplastic anemia in patients with sickle cell anemia is: Parvovirus B19
A 25 year-old African-American male with sickle cell anemia has sudden onset of bone pain. What is the management for the pain crisis?
Oxygen (O2)
Analgesia
Hydration
Transfusion (if severe)
What is the significant cause of morbidity in thalassemia patients? What is the treatment?
Cause:Iron overload
Treatment:Deferoxamine
